steps.title: Identify the workload shift
steps.content: Infected workers “cease their share of labor” while uninfected workers “feed, groom, and transport them.”

steps.title: Connect the burden to the outcome
steps.content:
Choice B — need to compensate for reduced contributions within the colony while also caring for infected workers is burdensome to the uninfected workers. directly identifies the cause: uninfected workers must compensate for the infected workers' lost contributions while also providing all their care—a double burden that explains their shortened lifespans.

The unsupported-scenario alternatives involve uninfected workers becoming infected despite no mention of transmission or predator attacks that are never discussed. Choice D — average lifespan of T. nylanderi worker ants in colonies without parasitic activity typically falls well below three years, the range covered by the study. merely restates baseline data unrelated to the shortened lifespans observed in parasitized colonies.

That matches Choice B — need to compensate for reduced contributions within the colony while also caring for infected workers is burdensome to the uninfected workers..
